confirmatory_analysis.name: regime_stratified_gain_check
confirmatory_analysis.summary: Stratified selector gain by dimension and size bucket to validate post-selection robustness.

panels: Panel A: mean constrained latency by prior profile with 95% CI.
Panel B: constrained regret versus global static policy with parity line.
Panel C: feasibility violation rate with bootstrap CI bars.
paper/figures/h2_selector_primary_panels.variables: Latency in ms; regret in ms (selector minus global static); violation rate in [0,1].
paper/figures/h2_selector_primary_panels.takeaways: Selector reduces latency and keeps regret near or below zero across tested profiles while maintaining low violations.
paper/figures/h2_selector_primary_panels.uncertainty: Error bars use 95% bootstrap confidence intervals across seeds.

panels: Panel A: distribution of delta_global = RG - RZ.
Panel B: seed-level scatter of selector risk against global risk with parity line.
paper/figures/h2_policy_dominance_panels.variables: RZ and RG are mean per-seed risk estimates in ms.
paper/figures/h2_policy_dominance_panels.takeaways: Positive delta_global values support weak dominance and frequent strict dominance.
paper/figures/h2_policy_dominance_panels.uncertainty: Seed-wise variability visualized; numeric CI reported in policy_class_dominance.csv.

panels: Panel A: empirical regret versus theoretical bound with parity frontier.
Panel B: per-seed bound satisfaction rate against 95% target.
paper/figures/h2_bound_calibration_panel.variables: Regret and bound in ms; satisfaction rate in [0,1].
paper/figures/h2_bound_calibration_panel.takeaways: Empirical regret remains below theoretical bound for the majority of tested cells.
paper/figures/h2_bound_calibration_panel.uncertainty: Rates vary by seed and are accompanied by table-level variability stats.

panels: Panel A: per-check pass rate for theorem assumptions (finite sets, positive priors, non-empty feasibility).
Panel B: counterexample detection confusion matrix for strict vs weak/equality boundaries.
paper/figures/h2_assumption_counterexamp.variables: Pass rate in [0,1]; boundary classes for predicted/actual strict-dominance behavior.
paper/figures/h2_assumption_counterexamp.takeaways: Assumption checks remain high while boundary behavior is explicitly audited for counterexample handling.
paper/figures/h2_assumption_counterexamp.uncertainty: Pass-rate variability is seed-aggregated and matrix counts reflect detection variability across runs.
